data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/12/25 – Futures Rise ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed Wednesday as investors awaited the release of the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. This key inflation report will provide valuable insights into the Federal Reserve's future monetary policy. Meanwhile, futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.36%, 0.21%, and 0.31%, respectively, at 4:29 a.m. EST, March 12.

Tuesday's trading session saw major indices decline, with the Dow Jones, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) all closing lower by 1.1%, 0.8%, and 0.2%, respectively. The market's reaction reflects ongoing concerns about trade policy and its potential impact on economic growth.

Adding to market worries was President Donald Trump's plans to double tariffs to 50% on Canadian steel and aluminum in response to Ontario’s 25% tariff on electricity exports to the U.S. Later, a Trump advisor clarified the higher rate would not apply after Ontario agreed to drop the extra charge.

Looking ahead, Wednesday's CPI report is a key focus for investors. Economists anticipate a 0.3% monthly increase and a 2.9% year-over-year rise in headline inflation.

The Fed is currently navigating a complex scenario, managing inflation while dealing with the potential economic impact of tariffs. The market is closely watching for signs of whether the central bank will maintain its current stance or consider interest rate cuts.

On the earnings front, Adobe (ADBE), UiPath (PATH), ZIM Integrated Shipping (ZIM), Crown Castle (CCI), and SentinelOne (S) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers today.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.274%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $66.68 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened higher today, following the European Union’s announcement of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminum imports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed on Wednesday

Asia-Pacific indices were mixed today, as Wall Street's slump as global tariff concerns weighed on investor sentiment.

Notably, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was down 0.76%. Further, China's Shenzhen Component and Shanghai Composite indices declined 0.17% and 0.23%, respectively.  However, Japan's Nikkei and Topix indices closed higher by 0.07% and 0.91%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar. 

Disclosure

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings Tomorrow. Here's What to Expect
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2024 on Thursday, March 13. The company develops quantum computing systems, software, and services. QBTS stock has rallied more than 192% over the past year but is down 36% year to date due to the rising competition in the quantum computing space and macro uncertainties. Analysts expect D-Wave Quantum to report an adjusted loss per share of $0.06 in Q4 2024 on revenue of $2.23 million.

In comparison, the company reported an adjusted loss per share of $0.06 on revenue of $2.9 million in the prior-year quarter. While D-Wave's bookings declined 22% in Q3 2024, the company said that it expects fourth-quarter bookings to improve on a sequential basis.

D-Wave Quantum claims to be the world's first commercial supplier of quantum computers and the only company that builds both annealing and gate-model quantum computers.

Analysts' Views Ahead of D-Wave Quantum's Q4 Earnings

Analysts are upbeat about D-Wave Quantum's prospects due to the growing interest in the quantum computing space.

Recently, B. Riley analyst Craig Ellis increased the price target for QBTS stock to $11 from $9 and reiterated a Buy rating. The five-star analyst believes that the addition of Microsoft’s (MSFT) “Quantum Ready” program to the Azure Quantum platform and the tech giant’s belief that 2025 will be “the year to become quantum-ready” could be potentially significant for the sentiment on the quantum computing sector.

Further, on Monday, Craig-Hallum analyst Richard Shannon reiterated a Buy rating on QBTS stock, reflecting his confidence in the company’s growth prospects.

Options Traders Anticipate Major Move on QBTS' Q4 Earnings

Using TipRanks' Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. The expected earnings move is determined by calculating the at-the-money straddle of the options closest to expiration after the earnings announcement. If this sounds complicated, don't worry, the Options tool does this for you.

Indeed, it currently says that options traders are expecting about 15.4% move in either direction in reaction to D-Wave Quantum's Q4 2024 results.

Is QBTS Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on D-Wave Quantum stock based on five unanimous Buys. The average QBTS stock price target of $8.63 implies 60.4% upside potential.

data.title: Alphabet's Google Revives Push into Smart Glasses with AdHawk Acquisition
data.description:
Tech biggie Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google is reviving its push into the smart glasses segment, with a $115 million acquisition of AdHawk Microsystems. Canada-based AdHawk provides eye-tracking technology featuring low-power components that enable effective analysis of a user's corneas and pupils quicker than rival sensors. The news was first reported by Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

What Is AdHawk Microsystems?

AdHawk was founded in 2017 and manufactures the necessary software, hardware, and chips that determine where a user is looking. The startup also launched its own glasses called MindLink, while it also sells the technology to other manufacturers. Some of AdHawk's investors include Intel Corp. (INTC), Venture funds of Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), Sony Group Corp. (SONY), and Ray-Ban owner EssilorLuxottica SA (ESLOY).

Of the total consideration, $15 million is conditioned on AdHawk reaching certain performance milestones. Although the discussions are in the final stages, the deal could fall apart since the agreement is not signed yet. Also, the companies are looking to close the deal as soon as this week, if all goes well.

Google's Renewed Push Threatens Meta and Apple

Google's renewed push in the smart eyewear segment could be a big blow to rivals Meta Platforms (META) wide range of smart glasses and Apple's (AAPL) Vision Pro headsets. Google's earlier attempt to create smart glasses (Glass) failed miserably as it failed to garner enough consumer attention and was discontinued in 2023. Interestingly, Meta had also pursued acquiring AdHawk in 2022 but the deal did not go through. An effective eye-tracking mechanism is the corner stone in building accurate Augmented Reality (AR) and vision devices.

Google has also partnered with Samsung in developing its upcoming Moohan headset, loaded with Google's Android XR. The software allows developers to use its framework to integrate eye-tracking support for their apps. The AdHawk team will reportedly join Google's Android XR team, once the deal is completed. Since its last failure, Google has promised to return to the smart glasses segment with the addition of AI (artificial intelligence). The acquisition of AdHawk backed by its superior quality, low latency, high speed, and minimal compute technology could be just the right step in that direction.

Is Google Stock Good to Buy?

Analysts remain divided on Alphabet's stock trajectory. On TipRanks, GOOGL stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 25 Buys and 11 Hold ratings. The average Alphabet Class A price target of $215.88 implies 31.6% upside potential from current levels. In the past year, GOOGL stock has gained 19%.

See more GOOGL analyst ratings

data.title: Adobe (ADBE) Is About to Report Its Earnings Today. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Creative software developer Adobe (ADBE) is set to release its first quarter Fiscal 2025 financials today. ADBE stock has lost over 19% in the past year, primarily due to uncertainty around monetizing its AI products, rising competition in the creative software market, and tough economic conditions hurting its Digital Media business. Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $4.97 per share for Q1, up 11% year-over-year.

Meanwhile, analysts expect revenues to increase 9.3% from the year-ago figure to $5.66 billion, according to TipRanks' data. Notably, Adobe has exceeded EPS estimates for the past nine consecutive quarters.

Analysts' Opinions Ahead of Adobe's Q1 Earnings

Ahead of Adobe's Q1 earnings, TD Cowen analyst J. Derrick Wood kept a Hold rating on the stock with a price target of $550 per share. Wood noted that though the recent survey shows better partner execution and growth in Adobe's small business segment, the outlook for 2025 remains weak.

For Q1 FY25, the analyst expects Adobe to meet or slightly beat first-quarter targets. However, they believe future guidance will remain cautious due to economic uncertainty.  

Meanwhile, RBC Capital analyst Matthew Swanson lowered Adobe’s price target to $550 from $590, while keeping an Outperform rating. Swanson expects the company to report strong first-quarter results on March 12, with the Adobe Summit taking place the following week. He expects Adobe to share more details on AI-related revenue and the growth of Adobe Express, a tool for creating graphics, videos, and web pages, to support Creative Cloud.

Options Traders Anticipate a Large Move

Using TipRanks' Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. The expected earnings move is determined by calculating the at-the-money straddle of the options closest to expiration after the earnings announcement. If this sounds complicated, don't worry, the Options tool does this for you.

Indeed, it currently says that options traders are expecting a 8.41% move in either direction.

Is Adobe a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, Adobe has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys, eight Holds and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $564.85, the average ADBE price target implies 30.25% upside potential.

data.title: Despite Revenue Setback, Arcturus Therapeutics (ARCT) Vaccine Breakthroughs Fuel Optimism
data.description:
Arcturus Therapeutics (ARCT) had a challenging fourth quarter, with revenue and earnings falling short of analyst expectations. Their annual revenue took a dip, dropping $14.5 million from the prior year to a total of $152.3 million. However, good news came in the form of European approval for their COVID-19 vaccine, KOSTAIVE, and advancements in cystic fibrosis and ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency treatments. Nevertheless, Arcturus has revised its 2025 projections, foreseeing EPS losses in the first two quarters while predicting revenues of $62.5 million and $70.3 million, respectively. The stock has seen a nearly 12% decrease since the announcement.

A Robust Treatment Pipeline

Arcturus Therapeutics is an mRNA medicines and vaccines company. Its therapies include LUNAR lipid-mediated delivery, STARR mRNA technology, and KOSTAIVE = the world’s first approved self-amplifying mRNA COVID-19 vaccine. It has an extensive patent portfolio, with over 500 patents and applications globally.

The company has recently made significant advancements in its mRNA therapeutic development, with two key products, ARCT-032 for cystic fibrosis and ARCT-810 for ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency, initiating dosing in Phase 2 studies as of December 2024. Results for both trials are anticipated to be released by the end of Q2 2025.

Further, the company’s sa-mRNA COVID-19 vaccine, KOSTAIVE, received approval from the European Commission in February 2025 based on strong clinical evidence from multiple studies. KOSTAIVE is now approved for use in all 27 EU member states and three additional European Economic Area countries.

The company has also initiated clinical trials for ARCT-2304, a new sa-mRNA vaccine candidate to prevent pandemic influenza disease caused by the H5N1 virus, with interim Phase 1 data expected in H2 2025.

Analysts Lower Price Targets

Arcturus reported a decrease in revenue for the fourth quarter and 2024. Total operating, research, and development expenses increased for the quarter and the year, mainly due to higher clinical trial costs associated with multiple programs, including COVID-19.

Net losses increased for the quarter and the full year, with year-end losses totaling $80.9 million. Despite the losses and decreased revenue, Arcturus’ cash position remains solid, with cash and equivalents totaling $293.9 million as of year-end. Based on current programs, the company’s cash runway is expected to extend until the end of Q1 2027.

Analysts have responded by lowering their price targets for Arcturus Therapeutics stock. For example, Canaccord and H.C. Wainwright have revised to $68 (from $74) and $60 (from $63), respectively. Despite this, both firms maintain a Buy rating for the shares. Canaccord noted that Arcturus’ 4Q24 earnings showed positive pipeline updates, and key programs remain on target. Meanwhile, H.C. Wainwright highlighted that Arcturus’ Phase 2 study of ARCT-032, a new drug in the pipeline for treating cystic fibrosis, is expected to hit its target by 2Q25.

Arcturus Therapeutics is rated a Strong Buy overall, based on the recent recommendations of seven analysts. The average price target for ARCT stock is $71.83, which represents a potential upside of 443.75% from current levels.

data.title: Analysts Underwhelmed Despite Vail Resorts' (MTN) Solid Q2 Performance
data.description:
Shares of Vail Resorts (MTN) saw a 7.6% increase for the day following the announcement of solid second-quarter results in fiscal 2025. Skier visits exceeded expectations with a growth of 6.8% despite challenging early season conditions, while earnings per share surpassed estimates. The company’s Q2 EBITDA of $458 million exceeded analyst and Street expectations. As for the future, Vail Resorts reaffirmed its full-year EBITDA guidance, indicating more solid performance on the horizon. Yet, analyst response has been less than enthusiastic, lowering price targets for the next 12 months.

Revenue Ascending

Vail Resorts is a major operator of mountain resorts and regional ski areas, with operations extending across the United States and internationally.

The company reported a net income of $245.5 million for the second quarter of fiscal 2025, an increase from $219.3 million in the same period of the previous year. Reported EBITDA was $459.7 million, factoring in $2.9 million in one-time costs tied to a two-year resource efficiency transformation plan and $0.1 million for acquisition and integration-related expenses. This marked an improvement over the previous year's EBITDA of $425.0 million.

The company has revised its guidance for the fiscal year 2025, with expectations for net income to be in the range of $257 million to $309 million. The EBITDA forecast for the year ending July 31, 2025, remains the same as the original guidance despite a $7 million impact due to foreign currency fluctuations. The updated EBITDA is projected to range between $841 million and $877 million.

The company’s Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $2.22 per share of Vail Resorts’ common stock, to be paid on April 10, 2025. The company also repurchased 0.1 million shares during the last quarter at an approximate total cost of $20 million.

Analysts Take a Cautious View

Analysts’ response to the recent announcement has been cautious in tone. Morgan Stanley analyst Megan Alexander has reduced the price target for Vail Resorts shares from $197 to $178 while maintaining an Equal Weight rating. She noted that the outlook by management, combined with expected investor uncertainty about visitation re-acceleration, may continue to affect the stock negatively.

Additionally, Barclays' Brandt Montour has also lowered its price target for MTN stock from $165 to $152 and confirms an underweight rating following the fiscal Q2 report, noting that the anticipated back-end improvement against the current unsettled consumer backdrop indicates that the outlook is not entirely risk-free.

Vail Resorts is rated a Hold overall, based on the recent recommendations of eight analysts. The average price target for MTN stock is $178.67, which represents a potential upside of 8.01% from current levels.

See more MTN analyst ratings.

Disclosure

data.title: Tesla Stock Jumps 4% as Musk Vows to Double U.S. Production with Trump Backing
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock bounced back on Tuesday, rising 3.8% to close at $230.58. This jump came after CEO Elon Musk promised to double the production in the U.S. and received public support from former President Donald Trump. The recovery followed a sharp 15% drop on Monday, Tesla's biggest single-day fall in almost five years.

Trump's Public Support Boosts Confidence

The rebound wasn't just about Musk's big promise. It also came as President Trump showed public support for Tesla and Musk. Trump bought a Tesla and displayed it on the White House lawn. This support came as Tesla faced protests over its political ties and recent vandalism targeting its facilities.

Calling the violent actions "domestic terrorism," Trump made it clear he stands with Musk and his company. This public backing gave investors a reason to feel more confident about Tesla's future.

Musk's Plan to Expand U.S. Production

Amid all of these developments, Musk announced yesterday that Tesla will double its U.S. vehicle output over the next two years. This move aims to strengthen Tesla's place in the market and ease worries about slowing sales in China and Europe.

"As a function of the great policies of President Trump and his administration, and as an act of faith in America, Tesla is going to double vehicle output in the United States within the next two years,” Musk said during an event at the White House.

Analysts Urge Focused Leadership

Despite the positive market reaction, some analysts express concerns about Musk’s involvement in political matters potentially diverting attention from Tesla’s core business.

One such analyst is Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities, who emphasized the need for Musk to concentrate on Tesla’s operations to prevent further brand damage. Ives said that Musk's absence from Tesla's factories and rising protests against his political ties have added pressure, with Tesla stock down over 50% since December.

However, Ives remains optimistic about Tesla's long-term innovation but sees the next few months as a key test for Musk's leadership. He maintained his Outperform rating and $550 price target on Tesla.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a neutral stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 12 Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $333.14, suggesting a potential upside of 44.48% from the current level.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Considers Using Natural Gas to Power AI Data Centers
data.description:
Technology giant Microsoft (MSFT) says it is open to deploying natural gas with carbon capture technology to power its artificial intelligence (AI) data centers.

In a television interview, Bobby Hollis, Microsoft’s vice-president of energy, said when asked about powering AI data centers with natural gas, “That absolutely would not be off the table.” However, he quickly added that using natural gas with carbon capture would only happen if it was deemed “commercially viable and cost competitive.”

Oil and gas companies have been developing carbon capture technology for years. Essentially, it captures carbon dioxide emissions from industrial sites and stores them deep underground. However, carbon capture at a commercial scale hasn’t taken off due to the high costs associated with such projects.

Ambitious Goals

Microsoft has big AI ambitions and big climate goals. The company has said it plans to spend about $80 billion this year building out its AI infrastructure, including on data centers. However, the company also has ambitious goals to address climate change, aiming to match all of its electricity consumption with carbon-free energy by 2030.

Last year, Microsoft made headlines worldwide with its proposal to use nuclear energy to power its AI data centers, going so far as to sign a deal to support the restart of the Three Mile Island nuclear reactor. Data center developers view natural gas as a near-term power solution despite its carbon-dioxide emissions.

Energy giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) announced last December that they are entering the data center space with plans to develop natural gas plants and accompanying carbon capture technology. MSFT stock has declined 8% over the last year.

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 31 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 28 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on MSFT stock of $507.54 implies 33.41% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on MSFT stock

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Billionaire Ron Baron Is Not Selling His Tesla (TSLA) Shares
data.description:
Billionaire investor Ron Baron is sticking with EV maker Tesla (TSLA) even though its stock price is down significantly from its highs. Indeed, the investor is so confident in Tesla’s future that he has promised to not sell any of his personal shares until he sells the shares he manages for his clients, and thinks that the current low price makes it a good time to buy more.

Baron has a good reason to believe in Tesla – it has made him a lot of money. In fact, he invested $400 million in Tesla between 2014 and 2016, which has paid off big time with billions of dollars in profits. As a result, Tesla is still a big part of Baron’s investment portfolio, and he’s willing to hold on to it for the long term due to the company’s innovative products and its leadership team.

Interestingly, Baron thinks that Tesla’s CEO, Elon Musk, is the key to the company’s success. Even though Musk seems distracted and is facing a significant amount of backlash for his government role at the Department of Government Efficiency, Baron believes that he is still fully committed to Tesla. In addition, he trusts Musk to keep making good decisions for the company, which is why he’s willing to keep investing in Tesla.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 12 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $333.14 per share implies 44.5% upside potential.

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Wedbush Expects New Highs from AI Stocks Like NVDA and TSLA in 2025
data.description:
Despite the recent sell-off in tech stocks, analysts at Wedbush believe that this downturn is temporary and that AI leaders remain well-positioned for long-term success. Indeed, they predict that these companies will reach record highs during the second half of 2025 thanks to the continued growth and adoption of AI technologies. Unsurprisingly, Wedbush’s top picks to weather the current volatility include Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Tesla (TSLA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Palantir (PLTR).

These companies have been significantly impacted recently. In fact, Nvidia has dropped 20% year-to-date, while Microsoft, Apple, and Tesla are down 9%, 11%, and 44%, respectively. Interestingly, Palantir has been a relative outperformer, with a year-to-date increase of about 1%, but the stock is still significantly off its highs.

Wedbush acknowledges that it underestimated the market’s reaction to the Trump administration’s policies. Nevertheless, it believes that policy uncertainties will eventually calm down in the coming months and present a “golden opportunity” for investors to buy into these quality tech names at attractive prices. It is worth noting that Wedbush’s calls on these tech companies are focused on long-term growth over the next one to five years rather than just the next few months.

Which AI Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the five stocks mentioned above, analysts think that NVDA stock has the most room to run. In fact, NVDA's price target of $177.41 per share implies more than 60% upside potential. On the other hand, analysts expect the least from AAPL, as its $250.95 per share price target implies 13.9% upside.

See more NVDA analyst ratings
